name: FII (Foreign Institutional Investor) Flows — India
summary: Created 2026-05-12. **FY26 FII OUTFLOWS NOW ~₹3.33 LAKH CRORE — THE CAPITAL IS ROTATING OUT OF INDIA INTO THE KOREA/TAIWAN AI TRADE, AND OIL RELIEF CAN'T REVERSE IT.** Day 90 (Thursday May 28): the FII channel — THE transmission mechanism in every India chain — stayed the binding negative. FY26 cumulative outflows are now ~₹3.33 lakh crore (Republic World May 28; the running tally has climbed from the ~₹1.92 lakh cr CY26 figure carried last brief), against a 15-year-low FPI ownership (~16%, below DII — DII 20.9% vs FII 17.1% in Nifty 500 = structural rotation, not just flow). The decisive read: this is not merely crisis-driven de-risking — foreign money is rotating OUT of India INTO the Korea/Taiwan AI trade (India -9% YTD vs Korea +90% / Taiwan +42%), so ~40% of the India weakness is a valuation-rotation that does NOT reverse on a deal, while ~60% is the crisis component that does. **Why oil relief can't fix it**: India printed a second straight down session despite operative Brent sub-$100 — oil-import relief is necessary but not sufficient when the marginal seller is structurally exiting an expensive (~24x P/E), FII-dependent market for a cheaper, faster-growing AI-semis EM. **Reversibility requires the FII flow to turn positive** — (a) Brent <$95 sustained 5+ sessions AND (b) FII flow turn positive AND (c) organic USD/INR ₹93-94. Operative Brent ~$99 is near (a) but (b), the binding condition, is unconfirmed and FY26 outflows are still climbing. DII SIP-led buying remains the only floor. PRIOR (May 27): **THE RELIEF SESSIONS DID NOT TURN THE FII CHANNEL — THE BOUNCE WAS PAPER, NOT POSITIONING, AND IT REVERSED.** FII flow is THE transmission mechanism in every India causal chain of the cycle, and over the ~48h window (no brief May 26) it stayed the binding negative: record FII outflow ₹1.92 lakh crore YTD 2026 (Republic World; already exceeds full-year 2025's ₹1.66 lakh crore), FPI ownership ~16% (15-yr low, below DII — DII 20.9% vs FII 17.1% in Nifty 500 = structural rotation, not just flow). The May 25 relief bounce (Nifty +1.32%) reversed when US CONFIRMED strikes inside southern Iran 'dampened hopes for a peace deal' — India gave back the rally May 26 (Nifty -0.49%) as FII outflows weighed and only DII inflows cushioned (the Nifty MidCap still printed a fresh high on DII absorption). **The mechanism / decision-relevant read**: oil-relief + the RBI $5B swap (~1.96x cover, healthy dollar appetite) + the 'undervalued' jawboning can cap the currency and bid the tape, but they cannot make FIIs net buyers — relief is paper, positioning is structural. **Reversibility requires the FII flow to turn positive** — (a) Brent <$95 sustained 5+ sessions AND (b) FII flow turn positive AND (c) organic USD/INR ₹93-94. Operative Brent ~$99 is near (a) but FII and INR both moved the WRONG way this window; (b), the binding condition, is unconfirmed. The markets dossier promotes fii-flows as the recurring transmission mechanism (E4) and is explicit that the crisis component reverses fast on a SIGNED deal while the FII/structural component does not. PRIOR (May 25): **MAY 25 WAS A DEAL-RELIEF SESSION (Nifty +1.32%, USD/INR firmed to ₹95.22, India VIX -7.9%) BUT THE STRUCTURAL FII OVERHANG IS UNCHANGED.** With US/UK shut for holiday, India's domestic-only session bid the deal-relief leg (Brent <$100 + US-Iran de-escalation hopes + RBI 'INR undervalued' jawboning) — yet the FII channel did NOT structurally turn: YTD CY26 outflow still tracking >₹2.19 lakh crore (already exceeds full-year 2025's ₹1.66 lakh crore), FPI ownership ~16% (15-year low, below DII first time ever / DII 20.9% vs FII 17.1% in Nifty 500), and DII SIP-led buying remains the only floor. **The relief bounce is paper, not positioning** — a single risk-on session lifts the index but does not reverse the structural rotation out of Indian equities; the markets dossier is explicit that ~75% of today's Nifty gain reverses on deal-failure + oil re-spike. The FOURTH fuel hike (₹2.61-2.71/L May 25, cumulative ~₹7.5/L) compresses RBI optionality and reinforces the FII-deterrence backdrop. **Reversibility requires the FII flow to turn positive** — (a) Brent <$95 sustained 5+ sessions AND (b) FII flow turn positive AND (c) organic USD/INR ₹93-94. Monday's relief session moved (a) and (c) marginally in the right direction but (b) — the binding condition — is unconfirmed. Tuesday May 26 ($5B RBI swap auction + Quad) is the next institutional read. PRIOR (May 24): **MAY MTD NET SALES ~₹25,896 CR PER GROWW/NSE (PACE FLAT VS PRIOR BRIEF). FII YTD CY26 OUTFLOW TRACKING > ₹2.19 LAKH CRORE PER PRIOR BRIEF. FPI OWNERSHIP ~16% (15-YEAR LOW, BELOW DII FIRST TIME EVER). FRIDAY MAY 22 DII +₹6,003 CR ABSORBED FII -₹4,440 CR — DOMESTIC INSTITUTIONS ABSORBING FOREIGN OUTFLOWS. THE FRIDAY DII/FII SPLIT IS THE CRITICAL SIGNAL: DOMESTIC BUYING IS THE ONLY FLOOR. IF DII BID FADES (MUTUAL FUND SIP FLOW DECLINES), NIFTY 21,500 (200-DEMA) IS THE NEXT TEST.** Monday May 25 first FII print of the Trump 'no rush' Sunday pivot is the operational test. **The non-mover Indian equities Friday despite Brent -5% weekly + INR strengthening + global AI rally is the cleanest demonstration that FII channel is held by structural sell-flow that does NOT reverse on intervention-driven INR retracement.** Reversibility requires (a) Brent <$95 sustained 5+ sessions AND (b) FII flow turn positive AND (c) USD/INR retracement to ₹93 (organic) — none imminent. PRIOR (May 23): MAY MTD NET SALES ~₹25,896 CR PER GROWW/NSE (PACE FLAT VS MAY 22 BRIEF). DII ₹6,003 CR ABSORPTION FRIDAY. CAPITAL-FLOW DIRECTION = STRUCTURAL ROTATION OUT OF INDIAN EQUITIES CONTINUES EVEN AS CYCLE DE-ESCALATES RHETORICALLY.** USD/INR ₹95.68 May 22 close (RBI ~$5B intervention drove rupee strengthening). **The non-mover Nifty 23,719.30 +0.3% 1D across the deal-anticipation window despite Brent -1.5% MoM + INR strengthening is the cleanest demonstration that FII channel is held by structural sell-flow that does NOT reverse on intervention-driven INR retracement.** FPI ownership ~16% (15-year low, below DII first time ever) unchanged. YTD CY26 FII outflow tracking ~₹2 lakh cr. **The Friday tape's FII-DII divergence**: equity (DII-buyers) vs FX (FII-outflows) is the cleanest India structural signal of cycle. **Saturday May 23 third petrol/diesel hike (cumulative ₹4.74 petrol)** is politically inflammatory pass-through that compresses RBI policy optionality and feeds back into FII deterrence. **Reversibility requires**: (a) Brent <$95 sustained 5+ sessions AND (b) FII flow turn positive AND (c) USD/INR retracement to ₹93 (organic, not intervention-driven). None imminent. **Sonal Varma (Nomura)**: structural AI-disruption-to-India-IT-services concerns + RBI restrictions on AD forex hedging increased FX hedge costs deterring portfolio inflows; sees further INR depreciation scope. PRIOR (May 22): **MAY MTD NET SALES ₹25,896 CRORE PER GROWW/NSE PRIMARY (BSE SECONDARY) — PACE FLAT VERSUS THE MAY 19 BRIEF'S ₹27,048 CRORE READING. THE STRUCTURAL ROTATION CONTINUES WITHOUT ACCELERATION; INR INTERVENTION-DRIVEN RETRACEMENT TO ₹95.69 DOES NOT REVERSE THE UNDERLYING FLOW.** May 20 alone: net sales -₹1,597 crore. **Camp B framing nuance** (per intel A3): on the kinetic axis, INR retraced (geopolitical relief read), but FII outflows continued, reserves bled, and the underlying structural bear holds. **The reversal is INTERVENTION-DRIVEN, not flow-driven**: RBI is paying ~$5B in reserves over 48 hours to defend ₹95.50-96.00, demonstrating that the rupee defense is now operationally explicit. **FPI ownership ~16% (15-year low, below DII first time ever)** unchanged. **YTD CY26 FII outflow tracking ~₹2 lakh cr** vs full-year 2025 ₹1.66 lakh cr — overshoot intact. **The non-mover Sensex/Nifty across the deal-anticipation window** despite Brent -5.3% + INR +0.78% retracement is the cleanest demonstration that FII channel is held by structural sell-flow that does NOT reverse on intervention-driven INR retracement. **Reversibility requires the FII flow to turn positive** — that requires (a) Brent <$95 sustained 5+ sessions AND (b) FII flow turn positive AND (c) USD/INR retracement to ₹93 (organic, not intervention-driven). **Forex reserves $688.9B week ending May 15** (-$8B from $696.9B) confirms structural drawdown rate; sustainability ~30 weeks at current pace, ~8-12 weeks in stress acceleration. **Bank Nifty -11.8% 3M = cumulative RBI defense MTM cost** does NOT reverse on crisis resolution. **Sonal Varma (Nomura) call**: structural concerns over AI-related disruption to India's software industry remain; recent RBI restrictions on AD forex hedging have increased FX hedge costs deterring portfolio inflows; sees further scope for INR depreciation. **The cleanest signal of structural FII bear case validity**: even after RBI ~$5B 48hr intervention pulling INR back ₹1.46 from intra-week low, FII flows did NOT positive-pivot. Intervention can cap currency but cannot reverse foreign positioning. PRIOR (May 19 — Monday): **DAY 8 (May 18): ₹96 TRIGGER FIRES — USD/INR ₹96.28-96.29 RECORD LOW; FII YTD ₹2 LAKH CRORE EXIT EXCEEDS FULL-YEAR 2025 BY ~₹40K CR.** FPI ownership ~16% (15-year low, BELOW DII for first time ever). **Reuters has ranked the rupee as the worst-performing Asian currency in 2026.** Sensex/Nifty open -1.30%/-1.04% Monday on Brent $111.47 (+2.0%, StanChart $130-145 path activates), Saudi drone interception (B1), Trump-Netanyahu coordination. **The cascade is now self-reinforcing**: Brent $111 → CAD widening → FII outflow → INR ₹96 trigger fires → RBI binary (defend/capitulate/administrative). At $111 Brent, FII channel will not turn — May 14 bounce fully reversed. **Reversibility ASYMMETRIC**: requires (a) Brent <$95 sustained 5+ sessions AND (b) FII flow turn positive AND (c) USD/INR ₹93 — NONE triggered, all moving wrong direction. **The RBI's weekly reserves print for week ending May 16 has NOT yet printed through Monday morning IST (lands Monday-Tuesday)**. **Tuesday May 19 Trump NSC binary**: strike order → FII channel cracks further (Nifty 21,500-22,000 test); back-off → potential FII bounce. **PRIOR (May 16): FII YTD ₹2 LAKH CRORE EXIT (EXCEEDS FULL-YEAR 2025); MAY FII RUNNING ₹14,231 CR OUTFLOW SO FAR; FPI OWNERSHIP ~16% (LOWEST IN 2 DECADES, BELOW DII FOR FIRST TIME).** **A9 RBI FOREX RESERVES — most recent data is week ending May 8 = $696.99B (+$6.3B w/w recovery from May 1 $690.69B)**; week-ending May 15 print not yet released. Yesterday's brief 'anything above $10B this week moves the sustainability question from theoretical to operational' specification remains a PENDING test. **A10 USD/INR — May 15 intraday high ₹95.9825 = 1.75 paise from breaching ₹96 Varma trigger (Day 4 of 5-7 session window)** — RBI direct intervention near ₹95.96 is the operational defence. **Sensex closed May 15 at 75,238 (-0.21%); Nifty -0.19% at 23,643.50** — Indian markets halted Thursday's bounce; late selling in metals and oil/gas; Tata Motors +5.14%, Infosys +1.92%, Tech Mahindra +1.79% (IT-pack rotation on US recession risk). Bank Nifty -0.8% / -11.9% 3M = RBI regulatory drag carries forward. **At $109 Brent, FII channel will not turn — May 14 bounce now under threat.** RBI primary-dealer trading volumes hike May 15 (annual targets for 21 bond market makers) is the liquidity-providing measure — India 10Y stayed flat-to-easing despite global yield shock. Government considering bond-tax cut for foreign investors (partial relief if announced). PRIOR (May 15): **DAY 5 (May 15): ₹2 LAKH CRORE EXITED INDIAN EQUITIES IN FIRST 5 MONTHS OF 2026 — SURPASSING ALL OF 2025'S ₹1.66 LAKH CRORE.** **FPI ownership now ~16% — lowest in 15-20 years AND BELOW DII FOR THE FIRST TIME EVER**. **$19B of that outflow since Iran war started — flow is overwhelmingly crisis-linked.** USD/INR ₹95.90 fourth straight record close; foreign safe-haven USD bid on Trump Iran ultimatum amplifies pressure. **Indian markets bounced May 14 after 4-session losing streak**: Sensex +789.74 / +1.06% to 75,398.72; Nifty +277 / +1.18% to 23,689.60 (pharma, healthcare and metals leading; Cipla, Adani Enterprises, Bharti Airtel top gainers) — but bounce is summit-pause-pricing on Trump-Xi joint statement, NOT regime reversal. **Government considering bond-tax cut for foreign investors — partial relief signal for INR; not yet announced.** **Sonal Varma's ₹96-within-5-7-sessions trigger Day 3 of window. RBI weekly reserves print due May 16 = next sustainability test.** PRIOR (May 14): Markets dossier C2 reversibility requirements unchanged: (a) Brent <$95 sustained 5+ sessions AND (b) FII flow turn positive AND (c) USD/INR ₹93 — none triggered. **FII (Foreign Institutional Investor) net cash-equity flows into Indian listed equities are the TRANSMISSION MECHANISM** in every India-market causal chain of the cycle. Tracked via NSDL/NSE primary daily prints. Sonal Varma (Nomura): 'Don't expect any sustained pick-up in portfolio flows, as structural concerns over AI-related disruption to India's software industry remain'; recent RBI restrictions on AD forex hedging have increased FX hedge costs, deterring portfolio inflows; sees 'further scope for the rupee to depreciate.' **DAY 4 (May 14): FII DATA VIA NSDL NOT YET RELEASED FOR MAY 13 SETTLEMENT — Jan-May 2026 cumulative ~₹1.97 lakh crore (Business Standard 'Personal Finance' note; March 2026 was largest monthly outflow in 6 years at $13.6bn).** Indian markets bounced 2 consecutive sessions on Trump-Xi summit-pricing (Sensex +0.07% May 13 / +1.0% May 14 morning intraday; Nifty +0.14% / +1.1%) — bounce is summit-pause-pricing, NOT regime reversal; cumulative drawdown from May 11 record still ~-1.83%. **C7 USD/INR ₹95.74 May 14 morning (₹95.31 May 13 close = 4th straight session fresh closing record); pace of weakening MATERIALLY SLOWED from May 11-12 collapse — RBI intervention sustaining defence at ₹95.50-75 zone.** ₹96 trigger Day 2 of 5-7 not breached. **RBI weekly reserves print due ~May 16 (Friday)** = critical signal — if extends above $10bn, intervention sustainability moves from theoretical to operational concern. **Markets dossier C2 reversibility requirements unchanged: (a) Brent <$95 sustained 5+ sessions AND (b) FII flow turn positive AND (c) USD/INR ₹93 — none triggered.** If Trump leaves Beijing May 15 without deliverable HEU framework, expect today's gains fully reverse Friday-Monday. **Watch Critical: Friday NSDL May 14 FII print — first POSITIVE day would be regime-shift signal**. PRIOR (May 13): **DAY 2 EXTENSION (May 13): FIIs CONTINUED NET SELLERS May 12 (multiple outlets confirm continuation rather than reversal of May 11 ₹8,437 cr outflow pattern); Moody's CUT India FY26 GDP 0.8PP TO 6.0% adds SOVEREIGN-RATING channel to structural FII case; Sensex closed May 12 at 74,559.24 (-1,456 pts, -1.92%) = largest 2-session drawdown of cycle; Hardeep Puri quantified OMC pain at ₹1,000 cr/day = fiscal-deficit channel quantified.** May 13 intraday relief bounce in Nifty/Sensex is technical not fundamental — cumulative drawdown -1.83% from May 11 peak. RBI reserves drawdown next print due ~May 16. FII (Foreign Institutional Investor) net cash-equity flows into Indian listed equities are the **TRANSMISSION MECHANISM** in every India-market causal chain of the cycle. Tracked via NSDL/NSE primary daily prints. **MAY 11 FII SINGLE-DAY NET SELL ₹8,437.56 cr (highest since April 24); DII +₹5,939.65 cr absorbed ~70%; YTD CY26 FII outflow ₹2.06 LAKH CR — already exceeds full-year 2025 ₹1.66 lakh cr by ₹40k cr (+₹14k cr in just 2 sessions May 11-12).** Drivers: Trump rejection of Iran counter-proposal + Brent $105.58 + INR breach of ₹95.50 RBI threshold. **The cascade is now self-reinforcing: weaker rupee → imported inflation → bond yields up (G-Sec 6.96-6.99%) → debt-FPI also out → more INR pressure → more FII exit.** DII absorption (~₹1.7 lakh cr YTD via SIP) covering ~70-90% of FII selling depending on day; gap WIDENING. FPI ownership of Indian equities at ~16%, lowest in nearly two decades. **STRUCTURAL CRACK POINT: if FII outflows accelerate past ₹2.5 lakh cr by end-Q2, DII channel cracks.** Sonal Varma (Nomura): 'Don't expect any sustained pick-up in portfolio flows, as structural concerns over AI-related disruption to India's software industry remain'; recent RBI restrictions on AD forex hedging have increased FX hedge costs, deterring portfolio inflows; sees 'further scope for the rupee to depreciate.'

recent_signals.headline: **FY26 FII OUTFLOWS NOW ~₹3.33 LAKH CRORE — THE CAPITAL IS ROTATING OUT OF INDIA INTO THE KOREA/TAIWAN AI TRADE, AND OIL RELIEF CAN'T REVERSE IT.** Day 90: the FII channel stayed the binding negative in every India chain. FY26 cumulative outflows ~₹3.33 lakh crore (Republic World May 28), against 15-year-low FPI ownership (~16%, below DII — DII 20.9% vs FII 17.1% in Nifty 500 = structural rotation, not just flow). **The decisive read**: this is not merely crisis de-risking — foreign money is rotating OUT of India INTO the Korea/Taiwan AI trade (**India -9% YTD vs Korea +90% / Taiwan +42%**), so ~40% of the India weakness is a valuation-rotation that does NOT reverse on a deal, while ~60% is the crisis component that does. **Why oil relief can't fix it**: India printed a second straight down session despite operative Brent sub-$100 — oil-import relief is necessary but not sufficient when the marginal seller is structurally exiting an expensive (~24x P/E), FII-dependent market for a cheaper, faster-growing AI-semis EM. The RBI's deep macroprudential toolbox can cap the rupee but cannot make FIIs net buyers — relief is paper, positioning is structural. **Reversibility requires the FII flow to turn positive** — (a) Brent <$95 sustained 5+ sessions AND (b) FII flow turn positive AND (c) organic USD/INR ₹93-94. Operative Brent ~$99 is near (a) but (b), the binding condition, is unconfirmed and FY26 outflows are still climbing; DII SIP-led buying remains the only floor.
sources: Markets dossier 2026-05-28 C5 (capital rotation OUT of India INTO Korea/Taiwan AI trade; ~60% crisis / ~40% valuation-rotation; India -9% YTD vs Korea +90% / Taiwan +42%; oil relief necessary not sufficient)

The May 25 deal-relief bounce (Nifty +1.32%, USD/INR ₹95.22) reversed when US CONFIRMED self-defense strikes inside southern Iran 'dampened hopes for a peace deal' — on Tue May 26 India gave back the rally (Nifty -0.49% to 23,913.70) as **FII outflows weighed and only DII inflows cushioned**; the Nifty MidCap still printed a fresh high (+0.54%) purely on DII absorption — the cleanest demonstration that the floor is domestic, not foreign. **The mechanism / decision-relevant read**: oil-relief + the RBI $5B swap (~1.96x cover, healthy bank dollar-appetite, not stress) + the May 25 'undervalued' jawboning can cap the currency and bid the index, but they CANNOT make FIIs net buyers — relief is paper, positioning is structural. **Reversibility requires the FII flow to turn positive** — (a) Brent <$95 sustained 5+ sessions AND (b) FII flow turn positive AND (c) organic USD/INR ₹93-94. Operative Brent ~$99 is near (a), but FII and INR both moved the WRONG way this window; (b), the binding condition, is unconfirmed. The markets dossier (E4) promotes fii-flows as THE recurring transmission mechanism and is explicit that the crisis component reverses fast on a SIGNED deal while the FII/structural component does not.

recent_signals.headline: **DEAL-RELIEF SESSION (Nifty +1.32%, USD/INR ₹95.22) BID THE INDEX BUT DID NOT TURN THE FII CHANNEL — THE BOUNCE IS PAPER, NOT POSITIONING.** With US/UK shut for holiday, India's domestic-only session caught the deal-relief leg (Brent <$100 + US-Iran de-escalation hopes + RBI 'INR undervalued' jawboning), Nifty +1.32% to 24,031.70, USD/INR firmed to ₹95.22, India VIX -7.9%. **But the structural FII overhang is unchanged**: YTD CY26 outflow still tracking >₹2.19 lakh crore (exceeds full-year 2025's ₹1.66 lakh crore), FPI ownership ~16% (15-year low; DII 20.9% vs FII 17.1% in Nifty 500 — structural rotation, not just flow), and DII SIP-led buying remains the only floor. **The mechanism / decision-relevant read**: a single risk-on session lifts the index on paper but does not reverse foreign positioning — RBI intervention/jawboning + oil-relief can cap the currency and bid the tape, but cannot make FIIs net buyers. The markets dossier is explicit that ~75% of today's Nifty gain reverses on deal-failure + oil re-spike, and the FII flows / cumulative fuel pass-through / RBI reserve drawdown that hold the structural bear are all unchanged. **Reversibility requires FII flow to turn positive** — (a) Brent <$95 sustained 5+ sessions AND (b) FII flow turn positive AND (c) organic USD/INR ₹93-94. Monday moved (a) and (c) marginally right; (b), the binding condition, is unconfirmed. Tuesday May 26 ($5B RBI swap auction + Quad) is the next institutional read.
sources: Markets dossier 2026-05-25 Section C2 (relief-bounce-not-re-rating; FII/fuel/reserve overhang unchanged)
Business Standard May 25 (Nifty +1.32%, USD/INR ₹95.22, RBI 'INR undervalued')
recent_signals.verification: Nifty +1.32% / USD/INR ₹95.22 / VIX -7.9% relief session = CONFIRMED market data (markets dossier Section A + Business Standard); FII structural overhang unchanged (YTD >₹2.19 lakh cr, FPI ~16%, DII>FII) = CONFIRMED carried structural data (no new positive-flow print today); 'paper not positioning' + ~75% reversal-on-failure = CONFIRMED analytical read (markets dossier C2).

recent_signals.headline: **FII OUTFLOWS YTD ₹2.2 TRILLION — ALREADY EXCEED ENTIRE 2025 (₹1.66 trillion); DII OWNERSHIP NOW SURPASSES FII (20.9% vs 17.1% IN NIFTY 500).** Through first week of May, FPIs withdrew ₹1.92 lakh crore from Indian equities (Republic World); April alone saw ₹60,847 crore outflow. **DII ownership in Nifty 500 now 20.9%, surpassing FII's 17.1% — structural rotation, not just flow.** The DII absorption of 'nearly 90% of outflows through strong SIP-led buying' is the technical reason Indian indices haven't capitulated despite the structural bear. **The Sensex/Nifty +0.4%/+0.3% relief rally is misdirection** — IT exporter names (TCS, Infosys, HCL +3-4.5% on weaker rupee) catch the postponement-relief while the underlying economy worsens. **None of the three Indian deployment conditions (Brent <$95, FII flows net positive, INR retracement to ₹93) is met. All three moving the wrong direction.**
sources: Republic World (FII outflow ₹1.92 lakh crore through first week May)
Intel dossier 2026-05-19 A13; Markets dossier C6
recent_signals.verification: FII outflows YTD ₹2.2 trillion = CONFIRMED (Republic World + Business Standard); DII >FII in Nifty 500 = CONFIRMED structural reading; SIP-led DII absorption ~90% of outflows = REPORTED ongoing structural pattern.

recent_signals.headline: FII SINGLE-DAY NET SELL ₹8,437.56 cr MAY 11 = highest since April 24. DII +₹5,939.65 cr absorbed ~70% (₹5,939.65 cr). YTD CY26 FII outflow ₹2.06 LAKH CR — exceeds full-year 2025 (₹1.66 lakh cr) by ₹40k cr; added ₹14k cr in just 2 sessions May 11-12. Drivers: Trump rejection 'TOTALLY UNACCEPTABLE' + Brent $105.58 + INR ₹95.54 fresh record breaking RBI ₹95.50 threshold + WH Sit-Room weighing renewed strikes. Cascade self-reinforcing: weaker rupee → imported inflation → bond yields up → debt-FPI out → more INR pressure. RBI forex reserves -$7.79bn week ended May 1 to $690.69bn = evidence of active intervention not stopping the move. Sonal Varma (Nomura) flagging 'further depreciation' on AI-disruption + RBI hedging restrictions deterring inflows.
sources: Business Standard May 11 + May 12 live blogs
Republic World / Outlook Money (₹2.06 lakh cr tally)
